back: Software used to detect, prevent and remove malware.

front: Give two types of anti-malware software.
back: Anti-virus and anti-spyware.
Encryption is a way of changing data so it cannot be understood, it cannot be read without the correct key.
To protect data during storage and transmission.
A protocol is a set of rules that allow computers to communicate.
HTTP is a protocol used to send and receive unencrypted data.
HTTPS is a protocol used to send and receive encrypted data.
SSL is a protocol used by HTTPS to encrypt data during transmission.
A firewall monitors and filters network traffic to block unauthorised access.
They control what users can see, edit or install on a system.
A proxy server acts as an intermediary between a user and the internet.

front: What can a proxy server do?
back: Hide IP addresses, improve privacy and filter content.
Settings that limit how software and websites access personal data.
Blocking location tracking, limiting cookies or disabling camera access.
The process of verifying a user's identity.
A password that is long and uses a mix of letters, numbers and symbols.
Using physical characteristics such as fingerprints or facial recognition to verify identity.
Using two methods to verify identity, such as a password and a code sent to a phone.
Protecting hardware from physical access or theft.
Locks, CCTV, ID badges, alarms and secure rooms.
Updates that install automatically to fix security weaknesses.
They protect systems from new threats without relying on the user.
